reviews.review_text: The team at BIG4 Mudgee Holiday Park are always a pleasure to deal with. Our staff always have a pleasant and comfortable stay here when working in Mudgee.  Would highly recommend 😊
Nice holiday park with nice staff and good services.

We stayed in a cabin which catered for our needs. While on the outskirts of town it was only 5mins into town. The cabin was clean and beds were comfortable although in May the cabin was a bit chilly overnight and the aircon got a workout. Staff were both friendly and helpful.

Very quiet road entrance. They only have two new 2 bdrm modern Cabins and some one bedroom new cabins. All other two bedroom Cabins are old and seen better days. There are no grass sites for caravans. You have to use credit card to use washing machines or dryer and there is a surcharge that they don’t tell you about. There is no bouncing pillow for the kids. They were not happy. A lot of small fifo cabins. The 2 bedroom cabin we got was small and out dated. Not the photos they show on their website. Very small shower. The second bedroom beds were not made. It would have cost $25.00 to get them to make it. Bit of a rort. The fry pan is old and food stuck to the pan so we went to WWorths and got a nonstick.May 2025. And here we are again on 16/2/26. Staying in the up graded 3 bedroom Cabin, do your self a favour and ask for this one. I have some photos of this one. Overall this upgraded Cabin is a 5 star. With a few minor changes. We bring our own salt and pepper grinders, and our own steak knives. And bigger knives. Because some cabins, don’t have them. The older casual staff member we spoke to. When we pre-booked 2 weeks earlier on the phone. Was absolutely customer service orientated and very easy to deal with and talk to.  We always pre- book over the phone. 99 percent of the time, it’s cheaper to book, than on line.
The new cabin is, luxury, romantic and modern. There are no USB connections. No bedroom side tables. No bed lamps in the master bedroom. It is a bonus having 2, 3 seater lounges. And a big coffee table. They don’t have a recycle yellow bin. So we take ours with us. To the next park, we stay in.
On my mobile phone in the cabin. Depending where you’re using it. Sometimes, you only get one or two Signal bars. Talking to someone on the phone, No problems. But using Google or anything like that. Getting connections, you may have to hit the try again button, two or three times, to get connected. At night I had to hit the try again button sometimes, five or six times. With my Telstra, you do eventually get connected.
In the single bedroom, with the window open. At 6am you can hear a heap of chooks in the distance carrying on. I’m happy to stay in this cabin from the 16th to the 19th. A very little bit of road noise, from 5am in the morning. With workers going to work. During the day, hardly any noise. Don’t forget Mudgee Golf Club for a feed of Chinese. The restaurant maybe closed on Monday and Tuesday. We were here back in May 2025. It was pretty delicious then. And packed on a big raffle night. We were lucky to get a seat. They also have takeaways. And the phone there, was busy too. The bedroom pillows were so soft, and very comfortable. There are no e-bikes being ridden fast in this park.

We stayed in the 2 bedroom cabin that sleeps 5. It was well set up and had a large fridge and stove with oven that was really handy. 2 car spaces out the front of each cabin. Games room, basketball court, pool (it was May and still warmish like 26 deg days) and playground were open and the kids liked those. It's well set up bbq and toilets in front of the playground area.  Highly reccomend. Would come back
No complaints

We stayed here with our small dog, they currently only have one "pet friendly" cabin. The facility was very good with a small grassy enclosure. I recommend staying here 👍
PS I never give 5 ⭐.. saving that for something spectacular..

Rude staff when checking in, set up caravan and an hour later they called to say they had made a mistake and we had to move the caravan to another site. Paid attention throughout the night, original site as well as many other powered sites remained vacant.

It was a different staff member who called to say we had to move sites to when we checked in and she was apologetic and friendly.  She did say the staff member earlier must've been in a rush to finish for the day, I guess she was right. Not a great experience
Très bonne expérience dans ce camping. Nous avons été très bien accueilli, le personnel et gentil et le camping très propre ! Je recommande vivement.
We stayed on a caravan site which was great
I LOVE THIS PLACE 😍 It Was Quiet We Were In Cabin 2 It Was The Best The Fun Games Room With The Fun Pool Time With This place I give I 5 stars 🤩 love it I Enjoyed the stay From Wednesday to Friday 😁

Very average experience. Cabins were old and not overly clean. The roads throughout the park are rough gravel so the kids couldn’t even ride their scooters. Minimal grass. A lot of gravel/tar. Felt like we were in an industrial caravan park, which it actually is in an industrial area. You just do not get a relaxed nature feel here at all vs other caravan parks.
The pool area is extremely small, overcrowded and poorly throughout.
A strange layout and location for a beautiful area such as Mudgee. We would not return, sorry.

Clean, well managed but dated & a bit of a walk to town (better to drive).
The pool is a winner on a >40C day!
Nice clean place . Easy van access to well marked sites. Well maintained toilets and showers. Friendly staff
Nice, friendly, helpful and clean, definitely good value

Really well laid out park, very clean, very flat for caravans, well maintained.
I would highly recommend and shall return.

Highly recommended.|Great facilities, including pool, table tennis, swing ball, air hockey, snooker table.|Great camp kitchen and BBQ.|Clean amenities, well maintained grounds. Tree cover or open sites. Level sites with concrete or grass pads.|Friendly, helpful staff.

If you're thinking of camping here without the need for power, dont.
$35 for a space behind the cabins in a no man's land. No facilities, water nearby and no trees, for shade or no anything. If you're a camper, I was in my campervan, they take your money and give you an empty space that looks into the neighbours yards. Not even a decent fence to give privacy to them or you.
Great place for a weekend for us. Cabin was clean and comfortable. Service was really good. The park was busy but everyone was friendly. Good spot to travel in and out of Mudgee.
Value for money,roomy cabin,pool was clean,good area,

Rude, disinterested, abrupt staff upon arrival, no excuse when you're in customer service.  Staff aware of severe effluent/water leak across our site but did not consider moving us to another site or even bothering to let us know.  Entire site smelled like mix of urine and dog poo.  Faux lawn matting waterlogged and feet sank down in spots, smelly mud ended up all in our new caravan and the ensuite. How dare you know there was an issue with that site and you still plonked us on it without any warning.  Don't care if your plumber is in Bali, use your brain it is not rocket science.  Inconsiderate.  Will not return will not recommend

Rude, disinterested, abrupt staff upon arrival, no excuse when you're in customer service.  Staff aware of severe effluent/water leak across our site but did not consider moving us to another site or even bothering to let us know.  Entire site smelled like mix of urine and dog poo.  Faux lawn matting waterlogged and feet sank down in spots, smelly mud ended up all in our new caravan and the ensuite. How dare you know there was an issue with that site and you still plonked us on it without any warning.  Don't care if your plumber is in Bali, use your brain it is not rocket science.  Inconsiderate.  Will not return will not recommend

We really enjoyed staying in the holiday park.  It was convenient spot, quiet and has lots of activities to do even on a rainy day.  I left my ipad in the cabin when I left and the staff in the park sent it back to me via express mail without any problem.  We enjoyed the town walk, the wineries and the natural beauty of Mudgee.

This park was nice & quiet with a good atmosphere. The play area was abit tired.  It was a little walk from town, but close to a little cafe, Harvey Norman & Bunnings among some other places. I would stay again..

The park was great for our family of six
We camped in an ensuite site and it was perfect for what we needed. Families were good around the park for our young family and the vibe was fantastic. Although on the edge of town it's only a couple of minutes drive to the town centre and an easy cycle if you'd prefer. Will definitely come back next time we are in Mudgee

Stayed here for two nights on an unpowered site. The area was adjacent a creek opposite a light industrial area. The log splitting business started at 7.00am and what sounded like a concrete jack hammering business not long after. At 4.00 in the afternoon they were still going strong at around 75db (yes, i measured it). Don't stay in this area or the adjacent cabins if you want any respite during a workday. I had trouble conversing with my wife due to the noise whilst sitting outside.  Amenities were adequate but location was a little out of town. Been to many much better van parks.

We had an extended stay here due to our car needing some mechanical repairs and they were so accommodating. A big thanks to Steve and Tracy and Tiana (I think Tiana is right). The grounds and ammenities maintenance were excellent keeping the facilities in first class conditions. It was a great place to be stuck with buses out the front and a good taxi service to and from the town.
An overall great stay.

We would like to say it was a lovely spot to 4 night  stay being our first time to Mudgee easy to get around our accommodation was fantastic bed very comfortable staff really friendly and helpful grounds people too our only mishap was  swarm of bees tried to move in but thats nature soon moved off and was offered another cabin if needed will visit again Pete and Georgie

The Mudgee BIG4 was excellent. We were provided side by side sites one night across the road from both the amenities block and the camp kitchen. Both were in excellent order and we enjoyed mixing with other guests as we prepared our dinner in the camp kitchen. There was an excellent coffee spot about 240m walk from the main entrance, but opening times were fluid. When open just another added bonus.

Lovely park mostly quiet.
Blowing leaves continually is very annoying. To top it off they are just getting blown back by the wind. What a waste of time . Otherwise beautiful spot.
Decent for the price rooms were clean and staff were nice
Clean and tidy for a caravan park great rate 5-10 minutes from main town and wineries

Absolutely fabulous, Tracey at reception went above and beyond to help.
Clean, tidy, pet friendly and spacious.
Would highly recommend a stay at the park.

Stayed 4 nights in the beautiful town of Mudgee in this Big 4 Holiday Park.
We stayed on an ensuite site, which is well maintained.
The ensuite itself was spacious, modern and clean, with a small heater included.
Reception was welcoming and professional.
Enjoyed our stay and recommended to others.

Location was a little bit out from the centre of town but it was quiet enough.
The amenities block was extremely clean.
Although the caravan park is a little dated, everything was in working order.
Overall it was a very comfortable stay.

Powder ensuite site 2 nights great site lovely park but biggest let down was lady in office so rude and very cranky over phone and when I arrived to get our site information all because I called her out on the advertised price and yes I was right they are  running a special winter price from $80 a night to $52 30% off till 21/8 if 2 night or more stay please double check your staff are updated on this as she then told me she don’t just make up pricing and rudely told me know where on any site on Google it was ever that price but then oh yes I was told Friday of this it’s only Sunday we arrived not a sorry or a smile very big let down for a beautiful spot

The big four at Margie was quite an experience. The staff are very helpful. If you have a problem just speak to the lady at the service desk and she will sort it out for you even though we did not have any problems and there’s plenty of places for the children to play and it’s a safe area.
Stayed in my van good park allows fires. Kids were just disappointed no jumping pillow like in pictures must been removed. Other then that great little park and kids made own fun!!! Would stay again.
We booked a 2 bedroom cabin, my wife and I stayed with some friends and went to the mudgee small farmers field day weekend.

We loved our stay here and found it to be really good value for money. The laundry facility is a tap and pay option. Fire pit and marshmallow toasting was a wonderful experience for our fam. The 3bedroom was more more than enough for our family of 4. Cabin 160 backs on  to Lions Dr. and surprisingly quiet.

This place is just great. The cabin was excellent, with everything you could need. The owners are friendly and helpful and it on the edge of town, so close to anything you need in Mudgee. I walked and drove around the property and there was a good vibe about the place. I turned right out of the place in morning one looking for coffee...I should have turned left! I only have a photo of the outside of the cabin, as I forgot to take photos inside before my luggage exploded all over the place.
Really. Nice hotel
Good caravan park

Both housekeeping and reception staff very obliging and friendly. Ensuite was large and the icing on the cake was the little heater in the ensuite. Heaven! The artificial grass between van and ensuite very welcome as the weather was inclement. Will definitely be back.

Great clean park. Staff friendly and helpful. Good size sites with room for those that want to stay hitched up. Clean and tidy amenities.  7 dollars card tap for dryers. Cheap rates sub 40 for powered sites if big 4 member. Short drive to town.

Great amenities pizza ovens bbq, playground. great sites large in size. Amazing camp kitchen. bins at sites and some very helpful staff. Close to town. Cheese company and wineries to visit. Lovely cafes. And some great pubs to eat at. Club courtesy bus.

The park is well set out with a nice pool for warmer weather.  We had an unpowered site with a caravan,  there were no powered sites available.  The unpowered area is fairly grassy but not an attractive area , looks a bit like someone's backyard . It is situated behind some cabins near a creek . It got really wet with puddles in the rain .

The park seemed very unloved , & was more suited for workers accommodation. Our cabin very noisy as right next to main road. Kitchen utensils very limited, made it difficult to prepare a meal.
Caravans were packed in tight. We watched as a number of vans tried to fit in the small spaces.
Would not recommend it for relaxing holiday accommodation- too many overnight workers & not a family park.

We enjoyed our 5 night stay at Mudgee Big 4, the cabin was located in a quieter area of the park adjacent to the Caravan area. The location is a good base for day trips and has easy access.

The cabin was clean, although more attention to detail is required by house keeping.

On arrival, We noticed after reception closed, there were no towels in the room.  There was a note in the compendium to call the after hours number at reception. i  walked across the park to reception in the inclement weather. I called the after hours a gentleman brought towels to the room.

Considering we were staying for 5 nights, we should have been allocated 4 towels instead of two towels and two additional toilet rolls.

The laundry facilities were easy to use, no coins required. There is a bar code on the machines / dryers that can be scanned with a smart phone, total cost of each load was $7.40.
Friendly comfortable cottages. We loved our stay highly recommend.
Clean, comfy,  quiet  place  for an  overnight stay
Comfortable, quiet and relaxing. Management are fantastic.
Park was brilliant, it was clean, well maintained and had the added bonus of a fire pit area.  Would highly recommend
Had family staying in cabin it was dirty, stove didn't work walls where dirty and marked. I stayed on non powered site with partner no water, no lighting ,no bbq or covered area.

I have been staying at BIG4 Mudgee on and off since Late February and have been very pleased. I routinely arrive later than reception is open but the staff have been very helpful in organising Keys and with any general enquiries or concerns I may have. The rooms themselves have always been neat and tidy. The kitchen area has plenty of kitchenware. The bathrooms have complimentary soap, shampoo and conditioner.
Great park , very well set out. Amenities are spotless. The staff are friendly and informative. We will definitely be back.

The park itself was great, lovely staff, clean enough facilities, plenty of areas to host groups of friends such as, covered outdoor bbq areas with seating ++ for that, nice big common indoor play area, nice outdoor activity settings, pool, basket ball court etc we stayed in a cabin (2 bedroom) the cabin itself is rather outdated within, could do with a refresh not the cleanest either…but that’s me being picky still very nice stay overall. Definitely recommend specially if travelling with children. Mudgee is a beautifully designed country town full of country charm and beautiful structures from the town clock tower to the towns numerous historical churches, clean and friendly people too. Lots to do and see around the area, wines, chocolate making, shopping, lots of scenery
Yeah, pretty sterile, dump point is long way from sites, in an industrial area so very noisy early from trucks etc nearby.

The park is quite nice but a long way out of town. On-site management quite poor and nothing seems to be replenished when it runs out.
We were unfortunate to have a very large group celebrating a number of birthdays and they effectively shut others out of the facilities. Took over the pool, the camp kitchen and were allowed to get away with load music and laughter through to well after 1 am Saturday morning and again very load talking until 12.30 Sunday morning, especially given the amount of signage decreeing a strict 10 pm curfew. Had absolutely no regard for others. If Big 4 (and other parks) want to cater to large groups then they need to add a fee for security to ensure park rules are adhered to, and a cleaning surcharge to have additional supplies and clean the amenities during the function.

Lovely caravan park.  There are approximately 32 caravan sites and each site is large and easy to get into.  The staff at Reception were very friendly and helpful.  The park is quiet and has so many things to do, have a look at the photos to see all the things to do.  The BIG4 is close to the GolfClub which also a wonderful Chinese Restaurant food was delicious.  Mudgee is a town  with good Pubs most with live music, eateries, shops and much more.  The BIG4 Caravan park is a great place to relax and a coffee shop close to the park which is a short walk and sells the best coffee I have had in a while.  A big thank you to the staff at Reception.  A short drive to the shops otherwise definitely would have given the extra Star.

Haven't stayed at any BIG4 park for many years but I always remember that those parks were of higher standard than most others and many regards. So I was quite disappointed when we checked into our cabin in this park. Starting with the location - located on the edge of a semi industrial area, I was not pumped. Most of the cabins, at least in the section where we were, are really old and outdated - hence I feel very overpriced for what they are. The overall look and feel of the park is very average, and its noisy - not sure why but it seems noise from several cabins away sounds like as if someone was knocking and stomping right on top of our cabin. Anyway - certainly not a place I would recommend for light sleepers. Didn't enjoy the area, the park and the facility at all, so would not be back and not necessarily recommend either.

I was a solo person looking for clean accommodation in mudgee without shared facilities (so no pubs). Honestly I had a really nice time! The room was airy, sunny, and most of all clean. The facilities with an internal gas stove were great, the staff also accommodated my late check in time coming from Sydney. A small balcony where you can enjoy some cheese and wine outside made it great. If you don't happen to like a soft bed it may not be for you, and I did travel off peak so it was quiet with not too many guests around, but if your looking for good accommodation with a pool you can't beat it! The wineries aren't too far away if you have a car otherwise book a tour and enjoy the local drop!

The park was outdated with dirty amenities. The ground was partly weed and some gravel and the sullage hose was hard to reach the drain as it was not nearby the site. All you could hear in the morning was roosters and machinery from a nearby business. The camp kitchen and the games room was dirty and I did not want my kids playing in there!

Won’t be back |We have stayed at a few big4 and this is the worst. Very outdated. Things they say on their website don’t have anymore. No jumping pillow, bbq broken, no pool table no sauna. | Sand around play equipment full of bull ants. |No cleaning stuff to clean dishes in room. Drinking glasses were dirty. |Very disappointed
Clean, dog friendly cabin with little yard. Very pleasant stay but a bit expensive with $ 200 a night.
Our stay was one of the best over our trip around the outback country. Mudgee Big 4 takes in all your needs and wants with having to compromise on anything.

Great amenities and well equipped cabin. Our visitors all commented how lovely the cabin was - we had a 3br renovated one that ticked all the boxes. We had everything we needed for our stay, except face washers/flannels which we did without. Staff were lovely. Bit of road noise from motorbikes and hoons in the main drag at times but that can't be helped. We will absolutely be staying here next time we are in town.

We stayed here for 7 nights on a powered site. The staff were friendly, helpful, and the park is well maintained.
Definitely recommend.

Enjoyed 2 nights with friends on adjoining powered van sites. Short walk to amenities, which were very clean. Not too busy. Sites are far enough apart so that we didn't feel crammed in. Very comfortable. Would recommend

Was lucky enough to find a cabin for the long weekend, however, our stay was marred by the park's poor amenities. Even though it had a decent pool, a few things were either out of order (like one of the 3 bbqs and the sauna) or falling apart (the pool table needs to be seriously replaced). Overall, the park needs a good refresh. The cabin was small (shower is tiny!!) and barely comfortable, but it's really outdated. There's still an old type clock radio next to the bed. Also, the walls were paper thin and i could hear my kid's moving around during the night. If you're coming, make sure you bring your ear plugs. At the end of the day I suppose you get what you pay for. Had stayed at the Gulgong tourist park during Easter in one of their modern larger cabins and would probably go back there in the future for the same price. Sorry, won't be back until this place has been brought into this century.

Noise from concrete business next door was very loud during daylight hours (7am till 6.30 pm).|Dont wash here. One load of washing was $7.48 (same price for drying) which is far too much.|Otherwise the powered sites are reasonably spacious and pleasant.

TOO NOISY
CAME BACK FOR LUNCH ON FIRST DAY AND THE BLOKE ON LEAF BLOWER WAS STILL REARRANGING LEAVES
IE MOVING SAME LEAVES FRIM PLACE TO PLACE
PERHAPS HE CLEARED LEAVES IFF EACH GREEN SQUARE BUT OVERNIGHT LEAVES ALL BLEW BACK
NOISE LEVEL WAS INTOLERABLE
WILL NOT BE BACK
IF YOU CAN’t come back to camp site during the day very poir
Anyone in a Blower pretending to be working is a joke
Ok if he was actually removing them but ih not jjst moved from one side to the ofher
I Won’t BE BACK

I told admin office on a number of occasions  that a shower cubicle has ran constantly  for over 3 weeks and will  probably  run a long time  after we leave. K They fixed it that afternoon , now I love this  park. Thanks BIG 4. K
Poor service  no reception 😢

Great place for a family friendly holiday
Facilities great and well maintained
Cannot say enough about this place please if you are travelling to Mudgee stay here just lovely

We stayed at Mudgee on the way through to Broken Hill for dog shows. We had 3 show dogs with us who were accommodated in their own trailer, picked up after & walked on leads.  We stayed in a one bedroom cabin which was very comfortable & clean.  Though our Waze navigator took us to the wrong place, Lyons lane, we found the park eventually & told the office staff who said it was a common occurrence. Office staff were fun, had a great dry sense of humour & very helpful when I needed some milk & gave excellent directions to the local Aldi.  All in all a great place to go. Not far from a great off leash dog park as well.
Pleasant park within 5 minute drive of the beautiful Mudgee golf course. Good facilities. Large part of park is cabins with long termers / FIFO.
Great place to stay when visiting Mudgee
Deluxe cabin was ideal for a few night stopover in Mudgee. Park was a great base for our stay
Beautiful park. So clean and well maintained. Would come back again.

Gee, what can I say. Not a bad place but probably the worst Big4 experience I’ve ever had! We chose this place because of other Big 4s were so good.

Arrived late in the day, in the rain, after an awful trip but we couldn't even get inside due to the gargantuan first step! What now??? Do we sleep in the car? Do we go home after our long drive to get here for a special event? My wife is experiencing some knee pain at the moment, and attempting to climb anything other than a normal staircase is impossible. That stair setup is seriously non compliant. How do I know this? I’ve worked in the building industry and that wouldn’t pass code anywhere.

It is extremely unsafe for anyone elderly or with mobility issues. Waiting until someone gets hurt and then finding out about compliance is not very wise. Anyway, our person at reception couldn’t help in any way but advised me to go to Bunnings to see if I could find something that I could use as a step and then closed up shop.

Well…I went to Bunnings and thankfully, did find a platform that I could use as a first step. A $100 later, I got back to the cabin and set up a new first step and helped my wife get into the cabin. Every time we went in and out, we had to set up a temporary step. Stressful, embarrassing and unsafe but there were no options. Let’s be clear. I wasn’t expecting a special disabled access or a ramp, just a normal, accessible, safe, staircase would have sufficed.

Can't tell anything from their photos. No bedside tables. Shower head fell off! At least the water pressure was good and beds were comfy even though you could barely walk past them. I’m not even going to talk about the other things, but I couldn’t I get the aircon to work and then what about the wifi? Geez forget that!

We couldn’t wait until Monday morning! Will never return and we come to Mudgee all the time.

Stayed in the cabins which had everything. Very comfortable & would definitely come back again. Loved it
Thankyou so much

Very very clean and maintained
Must visit with kids
Good
Location

spent three nights there during the Easter break.
The rooms were compact but tidy. The shower curtain needs to be washed, and the bathroom was really small. The water pressure in the shower was amazing 👍
The playground, swimming pool, and BBQ areas are conveniently located in the middle of the cabins, making it easy for parents to monitor their children's activity.

For starters, the kitchen's dry rack will come in handy for washing.
We had very pleasant stay for 2 nights. The place is quiet and near to everything. The rooms are a bit smaller but very clean and the cabins are well maintained.

Camp kitchen and BBQ sinks filthy and never cleaned by staff in the 6 days we were there.
They should be cleaned every day!
Overpriced for a nomad couple. It is possibly better for a large family. For us overpriced and could be maintained much better
A bit out of town, but that would expect for serviced camping site. Only few mins drive to town centre. We got ensuite power camping site. It is large and clean. Ensuite is well maintained and clean.
Good Clean Accommodation

Stayed for 3 nights at a powered site
Lovely shady location close to aged but clean amenities and pool area. Central for all our sightseeing. Disappointed they don't recycle rubbish but believe that is sore to previous clientele
Place needs a bigger kiosk, pool needs more chlorine. Definitely not a big4 park you'd expect. No jumping pillow for the kids, never see staff getting around like most other parks

Stayed a couple of nights for a work function. The cabin I stayed in was neat and tidy and a decent size (good for families). The pool was good (albeit freezing after a night's rain!) and we used the central bbq area. We were fortunate that we stayed mid-week so was fairly quiet. Close to town, we went and got pizza (Note: Dominos does not deliver here). The staff were friendly and welcoming. A great place to stay for a family getaway.

This was affordable and fantastic for the kids. A pool, a playground, a large sand pit, a games room and plenty of space to run around. It was quiet and respectful when dark and there was a lovely community feel. I stayed with extended family and we had 3 rooms/cabins and it was great. Great for family
Clean, comfortable and well equipped. Only downside was the unstable WiFi and the poor coverage with my mobile data (I’m with Optus)

Had a great time. Staff are friendly, amenities are very clean, pool was great.
Sites are really well laid out.
Will recommend to friends and family.

Be very quiet in the bedrooms. In our cabin the walls between the bedrooms were really no better than a curtain. 🤭🤭
We were there for a family reunion and had 4 cabins.
Suggestion to management that an inventory of kitchen equipment is done. We weren't lucky enough to get a dish drainer but could've made money on the kitchen tongs as, out of the 4 cabins, we were the only one with some and the others borrowed them every day.
We're used to staying in self contained accommodation but to not have any salt and pepper was annoying as that was the only condiment we didn't think we needed.
Two bedroom cabin with only 1 sofa and only 2 chairs on the deck?
All in all, a nice place to stay with good communal facilities for families and groups.

We had a lovely large and level powered campsite with artificial grass and gravel, plus filtered sun through the tall gums nearby. The bathrooms were clean and well maintained. There was a bit of road noise and no recycling facilities that I could find. Very good value particularly with a discount for multi days.
Toilets need new seats as they are in a bad state of repair
Pretty poor for the price. Looks nice, but not that good. Wouldn't stay again xx

Stayed two nights in one of the newer cabins.
Park was clean, reception staff very friendly and helpful.

Wonderful safe and secure place to be with extended family for a wedding event, both adults and children were accommodated for with activities and inclusion.   Many thanks for your easy booking in system and for all the positive communications links..   we shall return 😇
Clean cabins, friendly staff

Chalets tired looking, need refurbishment. Air conditioner needs upgrading, wouldn't cope in a hot day, heating component poor, needed to be on 30degrees to maintain adequate heat in chalet. Chairs fabric badly stained, needed proper cleaning /replacing. Needs security doors to be able to be locked (needed new key and barrel) Not appropriate in this day and age to have security door not lockable and then have company say they wont accomodate any loss. Previously to this accomodation 5 days earlier in a Chalet,(another location) anything worked easily.Location great. Staff accomodating.

Staff seemed to be kind. Two of the beds weren’t made. We had to do them ourselves. Kids jumping pillow was on park map but when we got there it is no where to be seen. So there wasn’t actually one there. When we unfolded sofa bed all dust, fluff and bits of food (biscuits, chocolate & sultanas) went absolutely everywhere. Obviously bed sheets on there haven’t been changed in a long time. And smelt very musty. Pillows were the worst pillows I’ve ever felt. Feels like the put some feather in there and called it a day. Floors were very dirty. Playground there is very very ordinary not much for a child to do there really… overall more negatives than positives. Was pretty darn ordinary and did not feel sanitary in the slightest. When visiting mudgee I won’t be going back there.

Presents nicely at the entrance
Staff very nice too
Cabin needs a good clean floor very dirty
Nice to visit
Powered site - good amenities- good pool - nice & quiet

The best shower,

definitely out of all accommodation I have stayed at . After sleeping at 6 different camp sites with cabins . The beds at Big4 Mudgee were the best. I actually felt rested .
Nice rooms, all clean, quiet area,plenty cabins and sites,playground for the kids and also a swimming pool
We enjoyed camping here! The service was amazing! Tracy was just so lovely. The camp kitchen was great to use and the amenities were very clean. We will definitely be going back here for sure.
Well set out park. Good facilities.  All caravan sites are large with plenty of room behind. BBQ area.  Tracy was great in helping us make our bookings for our club event next year
Nice tidy park, sites were good, plenty of shade.
Good location ,close to Town modern clean facilities
Super quiet, really comfy beds, everything you need in a caravan park cabin.

Not the cleanest park I've stayed in but not bad. Did find the pool and area dirty with the play ground a little out dated for a Big 4 park. Unfortunately the site we had was not level and the power and water was on the other side so had to run hoses cross the site. Dump point is not central to the park.
Little on the high price side for what the park offers as a big 4. Really not to fsr from the centre of town. Overall staff where very friendly and even stopped to welcome us there as is was a weekend when we arrived after hours.
Spotless well managed facility, we will be back and would have no hesitation in recommending.
The staff look after me very well

Lovely clean pet friendly cabin
My little dog loved his stay
Great utilities
Nice park. Well.kept.  Couldn't find any recycling bins though. A bit far from town but really a great stay all up⁷

Rooms and service were not what I have experienced in other Big 4 parks over the years.
Bed Room was OK a little small but we made it work. Living area not very big. We weren't replenished each day with tea or sugar and there is an ant problem. Bath room has no heating and no waste bin or sanitary bags and an ant problem.
I the second cabin we had booked the beds in the second room weren't made. When my daughter moved my granddaughter's pillow the was a big black spider under it. Some pest control would be handy.
Stayed in a caravan, so I cannot comment on their cabins. Sites are large and easily accessed.

Awesome town.
Great and clean amenities
N a s t y

From the first phone call you made my stay a great experience.

Cabin was newly renovated and it even had a dog bed, water bowls and an enclosed area for my dog.  I really appreciate your hospitality.  Can't wait to see it again when the renovations have all been finished.

The park is a little way out of town, however a local bus service runs past the front gate. IT has a well-equipped kitchen and a large, covered BBQ area. The sites are large and generally easy to access with a large caravan.
The amenities were clean, however there was no attention to detail. The paper towel dispenser in the men's toilets was empty for three days (and was still empty the day we left). I reported this to the management each morning and was assured it would be attended to, but it was not. The female manager spoke to me in an aggressive manner, as if it was my problem.
The sullage outlet behind our site was surcharging, so in effect raw sewerage was running out onto the ground. I reported this to the office on my first day and it was still unattended when I left 9 days later.
Additional to the site problems, the park took the final payment out of our account prior to our arrival without even notifying us.
I will not be back.

I had to cancel my accommodation because of a medical condition
I gave them 3 months notice but they still charged me 1 night's accommodation
So be aware they are money hungry

We stayed with a very large group  of people actually 27 of us.  Had tables set up undercover with extra large BBQ's  , we would all sit around  for happy hour , played trivia barbecued our dinner then would retire to our cabins .
The park is very quiet,  clean and the manager Stephen and staff were very accommodating.
We left very happy ,thankyou 😊

Comfortable in every way. Even has a walk-in wardrobe!
Good bed, good shower, good fridge, good kitchen. Quiet.
Excellent facilities, great roomy caravan sites, relatively quiet location. Highly recommend.

Wonderful park, very friendly service.
We highly recommend

Very clean and quiet caravan park. We stayed in our van with our two puppies. Had lots of walks around friendly people. Good size play ground for the kids. Would be good if a small dog leash free yard was available to let them run around a bit.  We have stayed there now 3 yrs for the Mudgee golf week  never had any problems  highly recommended accommodation ( just needs a dog yard )
Very clean, quiet and comfortable park. Love to come again

The staff and park itself is really lovely and well kept. The common room and the whole park was clean however we stayed in a 2 bedroom cabin which wasn’t as clean. The shower floods the whole bathroom floor even with the curtain closed, the glasses, plates and cutlery were filthy and we had to hand wash everything before use and the floor of the cabin was quite dirty too. We ended up leaving our shoes on all the time as our socks were getting very dirty from the floor. Otherwise the park was a lovely place to stay
Modern and well appointed cabins. Would stay here again.
Absolutely loved staying  here. The cabins were so clean, the service was fantastic great little holiday park for the whole family! Thank you again to management.

We stayed at the Big 4 Mudgee, as we were doing an early morning Hot Air Balloon flight with Balloons Aloft. This is a great location and well priced. They were happy to allow us late checkout to accommodate our balloon flight. We had an ensuite caravan site. Very new and well appointed.
Great park, would have given 5 stars if the was recycling evident
Cosy, good beds etc.
Was nice park but disappointed in the lack of communication about power being turn off
We stayed on a powered site with our caravan so can’t review rooms. Reception was an easy and friendly process- booking was easy, the grounds were well maintained and the amenities sparkling.
We had a dog,there was a dog bed and bowls and a fenced in yard.very clean,comfortable beds.great people that run it.

We had to leave on the Sunday 3 days before we were scheduled to leave due to me having a health scare. Would have been nice to receive a refund for those three days.
The residents in cabins were very loud. There was a large group when we arrived. The children  were up late playing soccer and yelling loudly. The second group were equally loud.
The laundry is outdated and expensive considering the state it is in. Some inconsiderate person put their clothes through the wash with a load of paper in it as was the dryer.
Good park well set out cabinscar comfortable
Very clean, all staff friendly and the amenities were clean. Great place not far out of town.
Great Caravan Park. Clean amenities, spacious sites. Good location.

Very clean cabin with good cooking facilities.Excellent lighting for reading.
Very welcoming and warm with heating turned on before our arrival. Much appreciated as the night temperature was -2 degrees.
Staying 4 nights in van. Very nice park and lovely staff. A bit far to walk to town centre.
Good size sites,gets noisey early in morning due to park being in industrial area. Park clean and staff friendly. Only downer was the timer on the light sensor in bathrooms was too short.
Half a role of toilet paper with no spares, dirty coffee mugs, no hand towels and dust. On a plus, the largest TV we have had in  any accomodation we have stayed in.

Great place to stay ! While travelling this was a clean cabin,quiet and plenty of activities to do with the kids.
Thanks Big 4 Mudgee
Amazing cabin! Affordable price! Lovely staff and location. 100% recommend
Great facilities, friendly staff
Easy booking, convenient after hours check in, great communication, nice accommodation and facilities. Recommend!!
Really enjoyed our stay great staff great amenities great position will go there again

Lovely clean bathrooms ... friendly staff
Will definitely stay again
